_meta.description: FareHarbor booking items mapped to website events. This file documents all bookable items in our FareHarbor account and links them to website events.
_meta.purpose: Central reference for staff to manage the connection between FareHarbor bookings and website content
_meta.staffInstructions: This file helps you connect FareHarbor booking items to website events. Each 'item' in FareHarbor (tours, camps, workshops) has a unique ID number. When you create a new event on the website, you'll add that ID to link them together. This way, visitors clicking 'Book Now' on the website go directly to the right FareHarbor booking page.
whenToUseThisFile: When creating a new ticketed event and need to find/add the FareHarbor item ID
When verifying that website events are correctly linked to FareHarbor
When troubleshooting a broken booking link
When onboarding new staff to understand the booking system

fieldGuide.id: The unique number from FareHarbor. Found in the URL when viewing the item in FareHarbor Dashboard.
fieldGuide.name: Friendly name for staff reference. Should match the FareHarbor item name.
fieldGuide.type: Category of item: tour, camp, workshop, event, lecture, experience
fieldGuide.bookingUrl: The complete URL visitors use to book this item. Generated from shortname + ID.
fieldGuide.linkedEvents: Array of event IDs from events.json that use this FareHarbor item
fieldGuide.pricing: Ticket prices in CENTS (1200 = $12.00). Match what's configured in FareHarbor.
fieldGuide.duration: How long the experience lasts. Match FareHarbor settings.
fieldGuide.capacity: Maximum participants per booking slot. Only include if limited.
fieldGuide.notes: Internal notes for staff. Not shown on website.
fieldGuide.status: active = bookable now, seasonal = only during certain times, archived = no longer offered

_aiRules.description: Rules for AI assistants editing FareHarbor item mappings
_aiRules.criticalRule: FareHarbor IDs must match real items in the FareHarbor dashboard
beforeEditing: ALWAYS verify FareHarbor item IDs with the user
ALWAYS check that linkedEvents match events.json IDs
NEVER guess or fabricate FareHarbor item IDs
NEVER change booking URLs without verification
validation: Does the FareHarbor ID exist in their system?
Does the booking URL match the ID pattern?
Are linkedEvents valid IDs from events.json?
Does pricing match what's in FareHarbor?
prohibited: Do NOT invent FareHarbor item IDs
Do NOT modify item IDs without user verification
Do NOT remove items without explicit permission

crossFileSync.eventsJson: fareHarborId in events.json must match id here
crossFileSync.bookingConfig: URLs should match patterns in bookingConfig.json

_aiRules.whenUnsure: FareHarbor IDs are critical for booking - always verify with user

_urlBuilder.description: How FareHarbor booking URLs are constructed

defaultUrl.description: When an event has requiresTicket: true but no fareHarborId, visitors go to the general booking page

lightframeEmbed.description: FareHarbor can open bookings in a modal (lightframe) instead of a new page
lightframeEmbed.usage: Already configured in the website. Booking links automatically use lightframe.

_howToLink.overview: Connecting a FareHarbor item to a website event takes 3 steps

step1_findItemId.title: Find the Item ID in FareHarbor
instructions: Click on 'Items' in the left sidebar
Find the item you want to link
Look at the URL in your browser - the number at the end is the Item ID
step1_findItemId.tip: You can also find the ID by clicking 'Share' on any item and looking at the booking link

step2_documentHere.title: Add the Item to This File
instructions: Scroll down to the 'items' array below
Fill in the item details (ID, name, pricing, etc.)
Add the event IDs from events.json that should use this item
step2_documentHere.why: This creates a central reference so staff always know which FareHarbor items exist

step3_updateEvents.title: Link the Event in events.json
instructions: Find your event in the 'events' array
Set 'fareHarborId' to the item ID (as a string): "fareHarborId": "562814"
The 'ticketUrl' will be auto-generated, but you can also set it explicitly

troubleshooting.bookingLinkBroken: Check that the fareHarborId matches an active item in FareHarbor. Item may have been deleted or deactivated.
troubleshooting.wrongItemOpens: Verify the fareHarborId is correct. Numbers must match exactly.
troubleshooting.noBookButton: Make sure 'requiresTicket' is set to true in events.json
troubleshooting.pricesMismatch: Prices must be updated in FareHarbor, not on the website. Website displays prices from events.json for reference only.

_itemIndex._description: Quick reference: Item ID -> Name (for fast lookup)

checklistBeforeCommit: Is the item ID correct? (Check FareHarbor dashboard)
Does the bookingUrl use the correct item ID?
Is the linked event ID correct? (Check events.json)
Is the status set correctly? (active, seasonal, archived)
commonErrors: Wrong item ID - booking goes to wrong event
Prices in dollars instead of cents - $12 should be 1200
Missing linkedEvents - item exists but not connected to website
Archived item still linked - visitors get 404 on FareHarbor

_changelog.change: Initial file created with all 2026 season items
